name: Real Sitio de San Ildefonso — circular desde el Embalse del Pontón Alto
region_intro: Touring cycling around Embalse del Pontón Alto features routes that traverse the varied terrain surrounding the reservoir. The landscape is characterized by the water body itself, with surrounding hills and the proximity to the Sierra de Guadarrama mountain range, which introduces significant elevation changes. Cyclists can expect a mix of paths near the water and more challenging ascents into the higher elevations. Best touring cycling routes around Embalse del Pontón Alto The most popular touring cycling route is Real Sitio de San Ildefonso — circular desde el Embalse del Pontón Alto, a 6.5 miles (10.5 km) trail that takes 44 minutes to complete. This easy route offers a scenic path around the historic site. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Fuente de la Reina – Fuenfría Pass loop from Parque Natural de la Sierra de Guadarrama Norte, a difficult 35.0 miles (56.3 km) path. This route offers challenging climbs through mountain passes. Local touring cyclists also love the Navacerrada Pass – Navacerrada Reservoir loop from Parque Natural de la Sierra de Guadarrama Norte, a 43.3 miles (69.7 km) trail leading through mountain passes and reservoirs, often completed in about 5 hours 33 minutes. Touring cycling around Embalse del Pontón Alto is defined by reservoir-side paths, mountain passes, and varied elevation changes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Embalse del Pontón Alto are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 1700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Embalse del Pontón Alto's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Embalse del Pontón Alto?
A: There are over 120 touring cycling routes to explore around Embalse del Pontón Alto, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of touring cycling routes in this area?
A: The routes around Embalse del Pontón Alto offer a mix of difficulties. While there are a few easy routes, the majority are classified as moderate (40 routes) or difficult (77 routes), often featuring significant elevation changes due to the proximity to the Sierra de Guadarrama.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes suitable for all ages?
A: Yes, there are a few easy routes suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1107603555/real-sitio-de-san-ildefonso-circular-desde-el-embalse-del-ponton-alto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Real Sitio de San Ildefonso — circular desde el Embalse del Pontón Alto&lt;/a&gt; is a relatively short and easy option, perfect for a leisurely family ride.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on the touring cycling routes?
A: You can expect diverse scenery, from tranquil reservoir-side paths to panoramic views as you ascend into the surrounding hills and mountain passes of the Sierra de Guadarrama. Many routes offer stunning vistas of the water and the natural landscape.

Q: When is the best time of year for touring cycling in this region?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for touring cycling around Embalse del Pontón Alto. The weather is milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be quite hot, especially in the midday sun, while winter can bring cold temperatures and snow at higher elevations.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its challenging routes, particularly those venturing into the Sierra de Guadarrama. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40018571" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de la Reina – Fuenfría Pass loop from Parque Natural de la Sierra de Guadarrama Norte&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31826077" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navacerrada Pass – Navacerrada Reservoir loop from Parque Natural de la Sierra de Guadarrama Norte&lt;/a&gt; offer significant climbs and rewarding descents for advanced cyclists.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes around Embalse del Pontón Alto are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40019797" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navacerrada Pass – Navacerrada Pass loop from La Granja&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option for those seeking a challenging ride.

Q: Where can I park when cycling around Embalse del Pontón Alto?
A: Parking is generally available at various points around the reservoir and near trailheads, especially in towns and villages like La Granja or close to the natural park entrances. Specific parking details may vary by route, so it's advisable to check the route description for recommended starting points.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Embalse del Pontón Alto?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 60 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the varied terrain, the stunning reservoir views, and the challenging mountain passes that offer a rewarding experience.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments along the routes?
A: Depending on the route, you will find opportunities for refreshments. Routes that pass through or near towns and villages like La Granja or Navacerrada will have cafes, restaurants, and shops. For longer, more remote routes, it's always best to carry sufficient water and snacks.

Q: Can I go touring cycling in Embalse del Pontón Alto during winter?
A: While some lower elevation routes might be accessible, winter cycling in Embalse del Pontón Alto, especially in the higher parts of the Sierra de Guadarrama, can be challenging due to cold temperatures, ice, and snow. It's crucial to check weather forecasts and trail conditions before heading out during winter months.

Q: What is the typical length of touring cycling routes in this area?
A: Route lengths vary significantly, from shorter rides of around 6.5 miles (10.5 km) like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1107603555/real-sitio-de-san-ildefonso-circular-desde-el-embalse-del-ponton-alto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Real Sitio de San Ildefonso — circular desde el Embalse del Pontón Alto&lt;/a&gt;, to much longer and more demanding routes exceeding 40 miles (65 km), such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40019709" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Cotos – Navacerrada Pass loop from Parque Natural de la Sierra de Guadarrama Norte&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: From this bridge that crosses the Pontón Alto Reservoir, there are fantastic views of the house of Santa Cecilia and Mount Atalaya.

Popularly known as La Granja, the Royal Site of San Ildefonso, declared a Historic Monument, is one of the best examples of the monarchical splendor of the 18th century. It is worth taking a walk around the place and enjoying the palace, gardens and its fountains.

An ideal place to practice sports and enjoy nature about two kilometers from the farm and offers one of the best views of the Sierra de Guadarrama. To access the dam we may have to overcome a few stairs.

waypoints.name: View of the Embalse del Pontón Alto and the Casa de Santa Cecilia
